files.patch:
Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_base.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_base.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_base.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+function __iwallpaper_base --description "Print the iWallpaper documents directory"
+    if set -q IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR; and test -n "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+        path resolve -- "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+        return 0
+    end
+
+    path resolve -- "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents"
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_dimensions.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_dimensions.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_dimensions.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+function __iwallpaper_dimensions --description "Print image dimensions for an iWallpaper file"
+    set -l image $argv[1]
+
+    if not test -f "$image"
+        echo "unknown"
+        return 1
+    end
+
+    if command -q sips
+        set -l width (sips -g pixelWidth "$image" 2>/dev/null | string match -r 'pixelWidth: [0-9]+' | string replace 'pixelWidth: ' '')
+        set -l height (sips -g pixelHeight "$image" 2>/dev/null | string match -r 'pixelHeight: [0-9]+' | string replace 'pixelHeight: ' '')
+
+        if test -n "$width"; and test -n "$height"
+            echo "$width"x"$height"
+            return 0
+        end
+    end
+
+    echo "unknown"
+    return 1
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_list.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_list.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_list.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+function __iwallpaper_list --description "List iWallpaper still images as tab-separated records"
+    set -l base (__iwallpaper_base)
+    set -l desktop_dir "$base/DesktopImage"
+    set -l image_dir "$base/Images"
+    set -l video_dir "$base/Videos"
+    set -l seen
+
+    if test -d "$desktop_dir"
+        for file in (command find "$desktop_dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.png' 2>/dev/null | sort)
+            set -l id (string replace -r '\.png$' '' -- (path basename "$file"))
+            set -l dimensions (__iwallpaper_dimensions "$file")
+            set -l kind desktop
+
+            if test -f "$video_dir/$id.mp4"
+                set kind live-desktop
+            end
+
+            printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n" "$id" "$kind" "$dimensions" "$file"
+            set seen $seen $id
+        end
+    end
+
+    if test -d "$image_dir"
+        for file in (command find "$image_dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.png' 2>/dev/null | sort)
+            set -l id (string replace -r '\.png$' '' -- (path basename "$file"))
+
+            if contains -- "$id" $seen
+                continue
+            end
+
+            set -l dimensions (__iwallpaper_dimensions "$file")
+            set -l kind preview
+
+            if test -f "$video_dir/$id.mp4"
+                set kind live-preview
+            end
+
+            printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n" "$id" "$kind" "$dimensions" "$file"
+        end
+    end
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_preview.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_preview.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_preview.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+function __iwallpaper_preview --description "Preview an iWallpaper image in fzf"
+    set -l image $argv[1]
+
+    if test -z "$image"; or not test -f "$image"
+        echo "Image not found: $image" >&2
+        return 1
+    end
+
+    set -l dimensions (__iwallpaper_dimensions "$image")
+    set -l size (stat -f "%z" "$image" 2>/dev/null)
+    set -l id (string replace -r '\.png$' '' -- (path basename "$image"))
+
+    echo "ID: $id"
+    echo "Dimensions: $dimensions"
+    if test -n "$size"
+        echo "Size: $size bytes"
+    end
+    echo "Path: $image"
+    echo ""
+
+    set -l columns 80
+    if set -q FZF_PREVIEW_COLUMNS; and test -n "$FZF_PREVIEW_COLUMNS"
+        set columns $FZF_PREVIEW_COLUMNS
+    end
+
+    set -l lines 30
+    if set -q FZF_PREVIEW_LINES; and test -n "$FZF_PREVIEW_LINES"
+        set lines $FZF_PREVIEW_LINES
+    end
+
+    set -l image_lines (math "max(8, $lines - 6)")
+
+    if command -q chafa
+        chafa --animate off --size "$columns"x"$image_lines" "$image"
+    else if command -q viu
+        viu -w "$columns" "$image"
+    else if command -q wezterm
+        wezterm imgcat --width "$columns" "$image"
+    else if command -q imgcat
+        imgcat "$image"
+    else
+        echo "Install chafa for inline terminal previews."
+        echo "Open manually with: open '$image'"
+    end
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_resolve.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_resolve.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/__iwallpaper_resolve.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+function __iwallpaper_resolve --description "Resolve an iWallpaper UUID or file path to the best still image"
+    if test (count $argv) -ne 1
+        echo "Usage: __iwallpaper_resolve <uuid-or-path>" >&2
+        return 1
+    end
+
+    set -l target $argv[1]
+
+    if test -f "$target"
+        path resolve -- "$target"
+        return 0
+    end
+
+    set -l base (__iwallpaper_base)
+    set -l id (path basename "$target")
+    set id (string replace -r '\.(png|jpg|jpeg|heic|webp|mp4|mov|m4v)$' '' -- "$id")
+
+    set -l candidates \
+        "$base/DesktopImage/$id.png" \
+        "$base/Images/$id.png"
+
+    for candidate in $candidates
+        if test -f "$candidate"
+            path resolve -- "$candidate"
+            return 0
+        end
+    end
+
+    echo "No iWallpaper image found for: $target" >&2
+    echo "Checked:" >&2
+    for candidate in $candidates
+        echo "  $candidate" >&2
+    end
+    return 1
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-set.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-set.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-set.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,62 @@
+function iwallpaper-set --description "Set an iWallpaper UUID/path and regenerate matugen colors"
+    set -l mode dark
+    set -l scheme scheme-tonal-spot
+    set -l target ""
+
+    set -l i 1
+    while test $i -le (count $argv)
+        set -l arg $argv[$i]
+
+        switch $arg
+            case -h --help help
+                _iwallpaper_set_help
+                return 0
+            case -m --mode
+                set i (math $i + 1)
+                if test $i -gt (count $argv)
+                    echo "Missing value for $arg" >&2
+                    return 1
+                end
+                set mode $argv[$i]
+            case --scheme
+                set i (math $i + 1)
+                if test $i -gt (count $argv)
+                    echo "Missing value for $arg" >&2
+                    return 1
+                end
+                set scheme $argv[$i]
+            case '*'
+                if test -n "$target"
+                    echo "Unexpected extra argument: $arg" >&2
+                    return 1
+                end
+                set target $arg
+        end
+
+        set i (math $i + 1)
+    end
+
+    if test -z "$target"
+        _iwallpaper_set_help >&2
+        return 1
+    end
+
+    set -l image (__iwallpaper_resolve "$target")
+    or return 1
+
+    wallpaper-set --mode "$mode" --scheme "$scheme" "$image"
+end
+
+function _iwallpaper_set_help --description "Show iwallpaper-set usage"
+    echo "iwallpaper-set - set an iWallpaper/MyWallpaper still image"
+    echo ""
+    echo "Usage:"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-set <UUID>"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-set <image-path>"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-set --mode light <UUID>"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-set --scheme scheme-expressive <UUID>"
+    echo ""
+    echo "Resolution order for UUIDs:"
+    echo "  1. ~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage/<UUID>.png"
+    echo "  2. ~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Images/<UUID>.png"
+end

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-rotate.fish
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-rotate.fish
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-rotate.fish
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+function iwallpaper-rotate --description "Pick a random iWallpaper still and regenerate matugen colors"
+    set -l mode dark
+    set -l scheme scheme-tonal-spot
+    set -l include_previews false
+
+    set -l i 1
+    while test $i -le (count $argv)
+        set -l arg $argv[$i]
+
+        switch $arg
+            case -h --help help
+                _iwallpaper_rotate_help
+                return 0
+            case -m --mode
+                set i (math $i + 1)
+                if test $i -gt (count $argv)
+                    echo "Missing value for $arg" >&2
+                    return 1
+                end
+                set mode $argv[$i]
+            case --scheme
+                set i (math $i + 1)
+                if test $i -gt (count $argv)
+                    echo "Missing value for $arg" >&2
+                    return 1
+                end
+                set scheme $argv[$i]
+            case --include-previews --all
+                set include_previews true
+            case '*'
+                echo "Unexpected argument: $arg" >&2
+                return 1
+        end
+
+        set i (math $i + 1)
+    end
+
+    set -l records (__iwallpaper_list)
+    if test (count $records) -eq 0
+        echo "No iWallpaper images found under $(__iwallpaper_base)" >&2
+        return 1
+    end
+
+    set -l candidates
+    for record in $records
+        set -l fields (string split \t -- "$record")
+        set -l kind $fields[2]
+
+        if test "$include_previews" = true; or string match -q '*desktop*' -- "$kind"
+            set candidates $candidates $record
+        end
+    end
+
+    if test (count $candidates) -eq 0
+        set candidates $records
+    end
+
+    set -l index (math "(random() % "(count $candidates)") + 1")
+    set -l selected $candidates[$index]
+    set -l fields (string split \t -- "$selected")
+    set -l image $fields[4]
+
+    echo "Selected: $fields[1] ($fields[2], $fields[3])"
+    wallpaper-set --mode "$mode" --scheme "$scheme" "$image"
+end
+
+function _iwallpaper_rotate_help --description "Show iwallpaper-rotate usage"
+    echo "iwallpaper-rotate - set a random iWallpaper/MyWallpaper still image"
+    echo ""
+    echo "Usage:"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-rotate"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-rotate --include-previews"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-rotate --mode light"
+    echo "  iwallpaper-rotate --scheme scheme-expressive"
+    echo ""
+    echo "By default, only high-resolution DesktopImage files are used when available."
+end

content.summary:
(b3)

After the matugen/iWallpaper integration work, user said: "I have wallpapers i've downloaded from MyWallpaper but i can't see them". Assistant investigated installed wallpaper apps and file locations. Key findings:
- The installed app is `/Applications/iwallpaper.app`, bundle identifier `com.macosgame.iwallpaper`, display/name strings include "iwallpaper" and localized "My wallpaper". The app is sandboxed and CloudKit-enabled with entitlement `com.apple.security.app-sandbox=true`, iCloud container `iCloud.com.macosgame.iwallpaper`, network client access, and user-selected read-only file access.
- Relevant app bundle strings include methods/keys like `GetCloudDownload`, `GetLocalDownload`, `downloadSubpaths`, `downloadDic`, `wallpaperRecord`, `wallPaperID`, `iCloudKitAssetPathKey`, `DesktopImage`, `Images`, `Videos`, `WallpaperWindow`, `setDesktopWallpaper`, `setWallpaperWithWallpaperRecord:`, `setDesktopImageWithWallpaperRecord:serialNumber:`, and file format hints `%@/%@.png`, `%@.png`, `.mp4`, `.mov`.
- The iWallpaper sandbox exists at `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/`. Important subfolders under `Documents/`: `Images/`, `DesktopImage/`, `Videos/`, `screen`, `tmp/`, `iCloudUserRecord`.
- `Images/` contains many UUID-named `.png` files. Most are low-res previews, often around `800x449`, `800x450`, `800x472`, etc. User later pasted a long `ls` from `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Images/` showing many UUID PNGs such as `01399C03-45C0-43D6-B4FF-49D4D1F19885.png`, `02C4B456-3620-49F4-B5D9-F422D988EB5E.png`, `REDACTED.png`, `7673EB9B-C822-452A-AD1F-1A58D9639338.png`, `B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7.png`, `C8CD821F-5AA5-4B8A-92C4-985440F8D085.png`, etc., and said: "I see a whole list it's not clear what i'd be setting...".
- `DesktopImage/` contains high-resolution stills for some live wallpapers. At the time of discovery it included `REDACTED.png`, `7673EB9B-C822-452A-AD1F-1A58D9639338.png`, `B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7.png`, `C8CD821F-5AA5-4B8A-92C4-985440F8D085.png`; subsequent listing during implementation found additional high-res DesktopImage files such as `3C7E37DC-CF8D-4B31-8CD3-F32F7143D280.png` and `4A6E0FE2-A27E-41EC-BDEE-853AD74D355C.png`.
- `Videos/` contains `.mp4` live wallpaper files with matching UUIDs for some images: initially `2115FC9C-8AE2-4A46-A5B1-1ACE298C2D09.mp4`, `REDACTED.mp4`, `4A6E0FE2-A27E-41EC-BDEE-853AD74D355C.mp4`, `7673EB9B-C822-452A-AD1F-1A58D9639338.mp4`, `B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7.mp4`, and `C8CD821F-5AA5-4B8A-92C4-985440F8D085.mp4`.
- Assistant clarified: `Images/ID.png` are previews/thumbnails, `DesktopImage/ID.png` are high-res stills for some live wallpapers and should be preferred for `wallpaper-set`, and `Videos/ID.mp4` are live wallpaper videos that `wallpaper-set`/matugen do not play; iWallpaper handles live video playback.
- Dimensions checked for key IDs: `REDACTED` preview `800x449`, DesktopImage `1920x1080`; `7673EB9B-C822-452A-AD1F-1A58D9639338` preview `800x449`, DesktopImage `3840x2160`; `B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7` preview `800x449`, DesktopImage `3840x2160`; `C8CD821F-5AA5-4B8A-92C4-985440F8D085` preview `800x473`, DesktopImage `3832x2268`; `2115FC9C-8AE2-4A46-A5B1-1ACE298C2D09` has preview and `.mp4` but no DesktopImage at that point; `4A6E0FE2-A27E-41EC-BDEE-853AD74D355C` has preview and `.mp4` and later a high-res DesktopImage was present.
- Recommended direct examples were:
  - `wallpaper-set "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage/B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7.png"`
  - `wallpaper-set "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage/7673EB9B-C822-452A-AD1F-1A58D9639338.png"`
  - `wallpaper-set "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage/C8CD821F-5AA5-4B8A-92C4-985440F8D085.png"`
  - `wallpaper-set "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage/REDACTED.png"`
- Recommended Finder/Quick Look command for visual choosing: `open "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Images"`; choose a preview visually, copy the UUID, then prefer the same UUID under `DesktopImage/` if present.

User said `:Mutagen` was not valid in Neovim. Assistant clarified that the real plugin is `matugen`; upstream `matugen.nvim` does not provide bare `:Matugen` or `:Mutagen` commands, only commands like `:MatugenWatch`, `:MatugenReload`, `:MatugenBrightness`, `:MatugenContrast`. User wanted the typo/command to work. Assistant modified `/Users/shahe.islam/neovim/lua/plugins/colorschemes.lua` inside the `daedlock/matugen.nvim` config to add:
- local `activate_matugen()` calling `vim.cmd.colorscheme("matugen")`.
- `vim.api.nvim_create_user_command("Matugen", activate_matugen, { desc = "Activate the matugen colorscheme" })`.
- `vim.api.nvim_create_user_command("Mutagen", activate_matugen, { desc = "Alias for :Matugen" })`.
Validation performed: headless Neovim showed `:Matugen`, `:Mutagen`, and `:MatugenWatch` exist; `:Mutagen` activates `matugen`; transparency remains intact. A recurring unrelated warning from Neovim bridge appeared: `Claude bridge failed to create state directory: Vim:E739: Cannot create directory /tmp/nvim-claude-bridge/0f0b341d: file already exists`, but the validation otherwise succeeded.

User asked: "Is it possible to sync these wallpaper images in exactly the same location cross Mac device with idrive?" Assistant answered yes, but advised not syncing the whole app container. Recommended syncing only source asset folders, not generated state:
- Sync with IDrive: `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Images`, `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage`, optionally `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Videos` if live wallpaper videos are wanted despite size.
- Do not sync the whole `~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper` container because it includes app state, CloudKit data, temp files, preferences, and device-specific records.
- Absolute paths are identical only if both Macs use the same username (`/Users/shahe.islam/...`); using `$HOME/...` is portable in functions. iWallpaper should be installed on both Macs first so the sandbox exists. IDrive may need Full Disk Access to read `~/Library/Containers`. Avoid opening iWallpaper on both Macs during an active two-way sync.
- Do not sync `~/.config/matugen/colors.json` because it is generated state; regenerate locally per device. Do not sync `~/.config/matugen/current-wallpaper` if each device should have independent selected wallpaper.
User agreed: "Yes I agree as i'd like to be able to modify different wallpapers for different devices dynamically". Assistant confirmed the model: sync source wallpaper assets globally; keep selected current wallpaper and matugen outputs device-local; each Mac can choose a different wallpaper from the shared library and regenerate its own `colors.json`.

User then asked: "The problem is with UUID, how do I know what to set? Or can i just set the wallpaper and the mutagen will automatically know what colors to set, or do i need to reference the UUID? Or would there be a way to rotate through wallpapers and have mutagen.nvim update my theme at the same time". Assistant clarified:
- Long-term, user should not manually deal with UUIDs.
- `matugen` only knows colors if given an image or source color. `matugen.nvim` only watches `~/.config/matugen/colors.json`; it does not inspect current macOS/iWallpaper wallpaper itself.
- If user runs `wallpaper-set /path/to/image.png`, wallpaper changes and `matugen` regenerates colors automatically.
- If user sets wallpaper inside iWallpaper/MyWallpaper directly, `matugen.nvim` will not know unless an integration/watcher is added.
- UUIDs are only needed because iWallpaper stores files by internal ID rather than title.
- Proposed helpers: `iwallpaper-pick` for visual selection, `iwallpaper-set <UUID>` for direct ID selection choosing `DesktopImage/<UUID>.png` first then `Images/<UUID>.png`, and `iwallpaper-rotate` for random rotation. Optional later launchd timer could rotate periodically. For live wallpapers, use `DesktopImage/<UUID>.png` stills for macOS wallpaper/theme palette; playing actual `.mp4` is iWallpaper's job.

User asked: "3. Show a visual/interactive picker, ideally with image preview. we support images in terminal don't we?" Assistant answered yes, likely depending on active terminal:
- Ghostty supports Kitty graphics protocol, good for inline previews.
- WezTerm supports iTerm2/Kitty-style image display depending on tool.
- iTerm2 supports `imgcat` / inline images.
- tmux image support can be flaky unless terminal and tmux passthrough are configured.
- Good preview tools: `chafa` (robust, works almost everywhere), `viu`, `wezterm imgcat`, `imgcat`, Kitty protocol tools. Plan: build `iwallpaper-pick` using `fzf` with preview command that tries `chafa`, then `viu`, then terminal-specific tools, with metadata/open fallback.

Current git/editing state before new implementation started:
- User had earlier explicitly requested no commits for the matugen/iWallpaper experiment; no commits or pushes were made.
- Dotfiles already had uncommitted matugen-related changes: `.gitignore`, `homebrew/Brewfile`, `scripts/setup.sh`, untracked `.config/fish/functions/matugen-generate.fish`, `.config/fish/functions/wallpaper-set.fish`, `.config/matugen/`.
- Neovim repo already had uncommitted changes: `lazy-lock.json`, `lua/config/autocmds.lua`, `lua/config/autocmds/styling.lua`, `lua/plugins/colorschemes.lua`, plus the later `:Matugen`/`:Mutagen` alias patch in `colorschemes.lua`.
- Runtime machine state included `matugen 4.1.0` installed in `~/.cargo/bin`, generated `~/.config/matugen/colors.json`, generated `~/.config/matugen/current-wallpaper`, and macOS wallpaper had previously been changed during testing.

parts.text: ▣ DCP | -299.7K removed, +52.2K summary — Compression #4

**Continuing implementation**

I need to keep moving forward. I’ve noted that the user provided a system reminder plan for building, so we need to implement that. I discovered a bug in the iwallpaper-rotate random expression that requires a patch, and further testing on the iwallpaper-pick preview might be necessary. I realize that in fish, if I define a function called `wallpaper-set` during the same invocation, it would override the autoloaded version. That sounds like a good way to test without affecting the actual wallpaper settings! I'm glad I figured this out, as it helps maintain safety during the process.

 function __iwallpaper_base --description "Print the iWallpaper documents directory"
     if set -q IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR; and test -n "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
-        path resolve -- "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+        if test -e "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+            path resolve -- "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+        else
+            echo "$IWALLPAPER_DOCUMENTS_DIR"
+        end
         return 0

-    path resolve -- "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents"
+    set -l default_dir "$HOME/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents"
+    if test -e "$default_dir"
+        path resolve -- "$default_dir"
+    else
+        echo "$default_dir"
+    end
 end
*** End Patch

parts.text:
**Clarifying limitations and usage**

I need to mention that the actual image preview in fzf works best in a terminal that supports image protocols. Otherwise, `chafa` renders images in a way that may not be ideal. We didn’t run stow due to existing conflicts, which is something to keep in mind. If the user wants to use it right away, Fish autoload should help because of the symlinks. If it doesn't work, they can run `source /dotfiles/.config/fish/functions/iwallpaper-pick.fish` to get started. I’ll provide a few commands they can use.

**Noting picker details**

I need to clarify that the picker rows display important information: the UUID, kind, and dimensions of the images. The `desktop` and `live-desktop` options are high-resolution stills, while the `preview` option refers to thumbnails. This detail is essential for the user to understand what they’re looking at when choosing images. It keeps everything organized and clear, which is exactly what I want for the user’s experience!

Implemented the iWallpaper picker/rotation workflow. No commits or pushes.

Added commands:
- `iwallpaper-pick` opens an `fzf` picker with image preview and sets the chosen wallpaper/theme.
- `iwallpaper-set <UUID>` resolves the UUID to the best still image, preferring `DesktopImage/`.
- `iwallpaper-rotate` randomly picks a high-res iWallpaper still and regenerates `matugen.nvim` colors.
- `iwallpaper-rotate --include-previews` also includes lower-res preview thumbnails.

Also added `chafa` to Brewfile/profiles/package mappings for reliable terminal image previews.

Usage:
```fish
iwallpaper-pick
iwallpaper-set B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7
iwallpaper-rotate
```

Validation passed:
- `scripts/test-filter.sh fish`: 52/52
- `scripts/test-filter.sh setup-syntax`: 4/4
- `scripts/test-filter.sh brewfile`: 2/2
- Non-mutating checks confirmed picker/set/rotate resolve the right high-res stills without changing your wallpaper during validation.
What would the workflow look like if we did this?

**Setting up wallpaper management**

For a one-time setup on my Mac, I need to install iWallpaper or MyWallpaper assets and sync them via IDrive. I'll ensure that `matugen`, `fzf`, and `chafa` are functioning. In Neovim, switching to `:Matugen` or `:Mutagen` enables the watcher.

For daily use, I can use `iwallpaper-pick` to select wallpapers and set them up with the generated colors in Neovim. I can also incorporate wallpaper rotation and cross-device syncing with IDrive to keep everything consistent.

**Clarifying UUID use**

I think I need to clarify the concept of "UUID hidden" for the user. It's important because the user doesn't necessarily need to understand it. The picker can visually select wallpapers without requiring the UUID to be displayed, and the UUID will only be set when it's copied. This way, the user can focus on the visual experience instead of the technical details. Keeping it simple seems the best approach!

**Workflow**
1. Run `iwallpaper-pick`.
2. Browse wallpapers visually in `fzf` with terminal preview.
3. Press Enter on the one you want.
4. The helper picks the best still image, preferring `DesktopImage/<UUID>.png`.
5. It runs `matugen-generate` from that image.
6. It sets your macOS wallpaper.
7. It writes `~/.config/matugen/colors.json`.
8. If Neovim is on `:Matugen` or `:Mutagen`, `matugen.nvim` reloads the theme automatically.

**Day-To-Day Commands**
```fish
iwallpaper-pick
```

For random rotation:
```fish
iwallpaper-rotate
```

For a known UUID:
```fish
iwallpaper-set B7647181-A222-4E15-9CD5-AD72B92A7FE7
```

**Cross-Mac Setup**
IDrive syncs the source wallpaper folders only:
```text
~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Images
~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/DesktopImage
~/Library/Containers/com.macosgame.iwallpaper/Data/Documents/Videos
```

Each Mac keeps its own local state:
```text
~/.config/matugen/colors.json
~/.config/matugen/current-wallpaper
```

So each device can use the same synced wallpaper library but choose/rotate independently.

**Neovim Flow**
- Use `:Matugen` or `:Mutagen` to switch to the dynamic theme.
- Use `:colorscheme tokyonight` to go back to the static default.
- When `iwallpaper-pick` or `iwallpaper-rotate` updates `colors.json`, Neovim reloads if Matugen is active.

**Future Automation**
Later we could add a `launchd` timer so your Mac runs:
```fish
iwallpaper-rotate
```

every hour, every day, on wake, or on login.
